title: quercus × organensis
intro: Quercus × reynieri (also known as Quercus × organensis) is an interspecific hybrid of Quercus robur and Quercus sessiliflora. This plant is a deciduous tree native to China, with its bark, leaves, and fruits being used in traditional medicine for various health benefits. The medicinal properties of Quercus × reynieri include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Quercus × reynieri, such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, are used to treat conditions like fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as to promote overall health and well-being.
